examples.title: Pseudocode: Page Replacement Algorithms Overview
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating FIFO, Optimal, and LRU page replacement algorithms with reference string processing
examples.code:
ALGORITHM PageReplacement(reference_string, num_frames, algorithm)
// Simulates page replacement with different algorithms
INPUT: reference_string (list of page numbers), num_frames (physical frames available), algorithm (FIFO, OPT, or LRU)
OUTPUT: number of page faults

BEGIN
 SET frames = EMPTY_LIST of size num_frames
 SET page_faults = 0
 SET queue = EMPTY_QUEUE // For FIFO
 SET lru_counter = 0 // For LRU tracking
 SET lru_table = EMPTY_DICTIONARY // page -> last_used_time
 
 FOR EACH page IN reference_string DO
 IF page NOT IN frames THEN
 // Page fault occurred
 INCREMENT page_faults
 
 IF frames has empty slot THEN
 ADD page to frames
 ELSE
 // Need to replace a page
 IF algorithm = "FIFO" THEN
 victim = queue.DEQUEUE()
 REMOVE victim from frames
 queue.ENQUEUE(page)
 ELSE IF algorithm = "OPT" THEN
 // Find page with farthest next use
 victim = FIND_FUTURE_FARTHEST(page, frames, reference_string)
 REMOVE victim from frames
 ELSE IF algorithm = "LRU" THEN
 // Find page with oldest last use
 victim = FIND_LRU_PAGE(frames, lru_table)
 REMOVE victim from frames
 END IF
 
 ADD page to frames
 END IF
 END IF
 
 // Update LRU tracking
 IF algorithm = "LRU" THEN
 lru_table[page] = lru_counter
 INCREMENT lru_counter
 END IF
 
 // For FIFO, track when pages were loaded
 IF algorithm = "FIFO" AND page NOT IN queue THEN
 queue.ENQUEUE(page)
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN page_faults
END

ALGORITHM FIND_FUTURE_FARTHEST(current_page, frames, reference_string)
// For Optimal algorithm - find which page is used farthest in future
INPUT: current_page, frames list, full reference_string
OUTPUT: victim page to replace

BEGIN
 SET farthest_distance = -1
 SET victim_page = NULL
 
 FOR EACH frame_page IN frames DO
 SET next_use = INFINITY
 
 // Find next occurrence of frame_page in remaining reference string
 FOR i FROM current_index + 1 TO length(reference_string) - 1 DO
 IF reference_string[i] = frame_page THEN
 next_use = i
 BREAK
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Replace the page used farthest in the future (or never used again)
 IF next_use > farthest_distance THEN
 farthest_distance = next_use
 victim_page = frame_page
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN victim_page
END

ALGORITHM FIND_LRU_PAGE(frames, lru_table)
// For LRU algorithm - find page with oldest last use time
INPUT: frames list, lru_table (page -> last_used_time)
OUTPUT: victim page to replace

BEGIN
 SET oldest_time = INFINITY
 SET victim_page = NULL
 
 FOR EACH frame_page IN frames DO
 IF lru_table[frame_page] < oldest_time THEN
 oldest_time = lru_table[frame_page]
 victim_page = frame_page
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN victim_page
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode outlines three fundamental page replacement algorithms: FIFO uses a queue to track insertion order and evicts the oldest page; Optimal (OPT) looks ahead in the reference string to replace the page that won't be needed for the longest time; LRU tracks the last access time of each page and evicts the least recently used one. The main loop processes each page reference, handling page faults by either using an empty frame or invoking the appropriate replacement strategy.

examples.title: Python: Page Replacement Algorithms Implementation
examples.description: Complete Python implementation of FIFO, Optimal, and LRU page replacement algorithms with visualization
examples.code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Page Replacement Algorithms Implementation
Implements FIFO, Optimal (OPT), and Least Recently Used (LRU) algorithms
"""

def fifo_page_replacement(reference_string, num_frames):
 """
 First-In-First-Out (FIFO) Page Replacement Algorithm
 
 Replace the page that has been in memory the longest.
 """
 frames = []
 page_faults = 0
 queue = [] # Maintains order of page arrivals
 
 print("=== FIFO Algorithm ===")
 print(f"Frames: {num_frames}, Reference: {reference_string}\n")
 
 for page in reference_string:
 print(f"Reference: {page} ", end="")
 
 if page not in frames:
 # Page fault
 page_faults += 1
 print(f"-> FAULT ", end="")
 
 if len(frames) < num_frames:
 frames.append(page)
 else:
 # Replace oldest page (first in queue)
 victim = queue.pop(0)
 frames.remove(victim)
 frames.append(page)
 
 queue.append(page)
 else:
 print(f"-> HIT ", end="")
 
 print(f"Frames: {frames}")
 
 print(f"\nTotal Page Faults: {page_faults}")
 return page_faults


def optimal_page_replacement(reference_string, num_frames):
 """
 Optimal (OPT) Page Replacement Algorithm
 
 Replace the page that will not be used for the longest time in the future.
 This is an ideal algorithm that cannot be implemented in practice but serves
 as a benchmark.
 """
 frames = []
 page_faults = 0
 
 print("\n=== Optimal (OPT) Algorithm ===")
 print(f"Frames: {num_frames}, Reference: {reference_string}\n")
 
 for i, page in enumerate(reference_string):
 print(f"Reference: {page} ", end="")
 
 if page not in frames:
 page_faults += 1
 print(f"-> FAULT ", end="")
 
 if len(frames) < num_frames:
 frames.append(page)
 else:
 # Find page with farthest next use
 victim_idx = None
 max_future_use = -1
 
 for j, frame_page in enumerate(frames):
 # Find next use of this frame_page in the future
 next_use = float('inf')
 for k in range(i + 1, len(reference_string)):
 if reference_string[k] == frame_page:
 next_use = k
 break
 
 if next_use > max_future_use:
 max_future_use = next_use
 victim_idx = j
 
 # Replace the victim
 frames.pop(victim_idx)
 frames.append(page)
 else:
 print(f"-> HIT ", end="")
 
 print(f"Frames: {frames}")
 
 print(f"\nTotal Page Faults: {page_faults}")
 return page_faults


def lru_page_replacement(reference_string, num_frames):
 """
 Least Recently Used (LRU) Page Replacement Algorithm
 
 Replace the page that has not been used for the longest time.
 """
 frames = []
 page_faults = 0
 lru_counter = 0
 lru_time = {} # page -> last used counter
 
 print("\n=== LRU Algorithm ===")
 print(f"Frames: {num_frames}, Reference: {reference_string}\n")
 
 for page in reference_string:
 print(f"Reference: {page} ", end="")
 
 if page not in frames:
 page_faults += 1
 print(f"-> FAULT ", end="")
 
 if len(frames) < num_frames:
 frames.append(page)
 else:
 # Find LRU page (oldest last use time)
 lru_page = min(frames, key=lambda p: lru_time.get(p, 0))
 frames.remove(lru_page)
 frames.append(page)
 else:
 print(f"-> HIT ", end="")
 
 # Update LRU time
 lru_time[page] = lru_counter
 lru_counter += 1
 
 print(f"Frames: {frames}")
 
 print(f"\nTotal Page Faults: {page_faults}")
 return page_faults


def compare_algorithms(reference_string, num_frames):
 """
 Compare all three page replacement algorithms
 """
 print("=" * 60)
 print("PAGE REPLACEMENT ALGORITHMS COMPARISON")
 print("=" * 60)
 print(f"Reference String: {reference_string}")
 print(f"Number of Frames: {num_frames}")
 print("=" * 60)
 
 fifo_faults = fifo_page_replacement(reference_string, num_frames)
 opt_faults = optimal_page_replacement(reference_string, num_frames)
 lru_faults = lru_page_replacement(reference_string, num_frames)
 
 print("\n" + "=" * 60)
 print("SUMMARY")
 print("=" * 60)
 print(f"FIFO Page Faults: {fifo_faults}")
 print(f"Optimal Page Faults: {opt_faults}")
 print(f"LRU Page Faults: {lru_faults}")
 print("=" * 60)


# Example usage with the reference string from the textbook
if __name__ == "__main__":
 # Reference string from the textbook example
 reference_string = [7, 0, 1, 2, 0, 3, 0, 4, 2, 3, 0, 3, 2, 1, 2, 0, 1, 7, 0, 1]
 num_frames = 3
 
 compare_algorithms(reference_string, num_frames)
 
 # Additional example: Demonstrate Belady's Anomaly
 print("\n\n")
 print("=" * 60)
 print("BELADY'S ANOMALY DEMONSTRATION")
 print("=" * 60)
 
 # Reference string that causes Belady's Anomaly with FIFO
 anomaly_ref = [1, 2, 3, 4, 1, 2, 5, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
 print(f"Reference String: {anomaly_ref}")
 
 for frames in [3, 4]:
 faults = fifo_page_replacement(anomaly_ref, frames)
 print(f"Frames: {frames} -> Faults: {faults}")
 print("Note: More frames can cause MORE page faults with FIFO!")
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates three major page replacement algorithms: FIFO maintains a queue to track page arrival order and evicts the oldest; Optimal uses lookahead to choose the page that won't be needed for the longest time; LRU tracks the last access time of each page using a counter dictionary. The code includes detailed visualization showing each step of the algorithm, and also demonstrates Belady's Anomaly where increasing frames can paradoxically increase page faults with FIFO. Time complexity: O(n²) for OPT due to future lookup, O(n) for FIFO and LRU with efficient data structures.

examples.title: C: Page Replacement Algorithms Implementation
examples.description: Complete C implementation of FIFO, Optimal, and LRU page replacement with detailed output
examples.explanation: This C implementation provides complete working versions of FIFO, Optimal, and LRU page replacement algorithms. The code uses arrays to represent physical memory frames and implements each algorithm with clear logic: FIFO uses a queue to track arrival order, Optimal looks ahead in the reference string using findNextUse() to determine which page to evict, and LRU maintains a last_used timestamp array that gets updated on each page access. The program includes detailed step-by-step output showing each reference, whether it was a hit or fault, and the current state of frames. Time complexity: O(n²) for OPT (due to nested future lookups), O(n×m) for FIFO and LRU where n is reference length and m is number of frames.
